description: Value investor. Buys durable franchises, holds forever. Concentrated book.
bio: Chairman of Berkshire Hathaway. Compounded book value at ~20% annualized for six decades by buying high-quality businesses at reasonable prices and letting time do the work.

description: Activist investor. Big, concentrated positions in mispriced large caps. Loud public theses.
bio: Founder of Pershing Square. Activist style — buys 5-10% of a company, publishes a thesis, agitates for change. Notable wins: Wendy's, Canadian Pacific, Lowe's. Notable miss: Valeant.

description: Contrarian short-seller. Made The Big Short famous. Now bets against bubbles big and small.
bio: Founder of Scion. Famously called the 2008 housing crash. Reads 10-Ks for fun. Disclosures often include large put-option bets; remember 13F shows notional, not direction.

description: Distressed-debt and large-cap macro trader. Aggressive, opportunistic.
bio: Founder of Appaloosa. Made his career buying bank debt in 2009 when the world thought they were going to zero. Owner of the Carolina Panthers.

description: Event-driven activist. Famous for combative letters to underperforming CEOs.
bio: Founder of Third Point. Multi-strategy hedge fund — event-driven, distressed, activism. The pen behind some of the most quotable activist letters in Wall Street history.

description: Thematic growth investor. Concentrated bets on disruptive innovation — AI, genomics, robotics, crypto.
bio: Founder of ARK Invest. Best known for the ARKK ETF. Bold long-duration bets on transformative tech; transparent daily disclosures of all trades. High volatility, polarizing track record.

description: Macro trader. Famous for outsized returns with low drawdowns over 30 years.
bio: Ran Soros's Quantum Fund — co-architect of the Black Wednesday GBP trade. ~30% annualized for 30 years at Duquesne, no losing year. Now manages family money.

description: Distressed-debt veteran. Writes the legendary Oaktree Memos.
bio: Co-founder of Oaktree Capital. Made his name buying high-yield debt before it was respectable. The Oaktree Memos are required reading; second-level thinking is the through-line.

description: Margin-of-safety value investor. Holds cash for years if nothing is cheap enough.
bio: Founder of Baupost. Author of Margin of Safety (out-of-print, $1k used). Famously patient — will sit in cash for years rather than pay full price.

description: Original activist. Buys big stakes, pushes for breakups, special dividends, board seats.
bio: Founder of Icahn Enterprises. The archetypal corporate raider turned long-term activist. Targets companies he believes are mismanaged or undervalued.

description: Long-short value. Famous shorts: Lehman, Allied Capital, Green Mountain.
bio: Founder of Greenlight Capital. Forensic accountant by instinct. Famous shorts include Lehman Brothers (2007) and Allied Capital. Donates winnings annually to charity.

description: Macro fund. Risk-parity, all-weather. Hundreds of positions across equities and ETFs.
bio: Founder of Bridgewater, the world's largest hedge fund at its peak. All-weather and Pure Alpha strategies. Author of Principles. Macro-first, systematic, deeply diversified.

description: Quant pioneer. The Medallion Fund — likely the best-performing fund in history.
bio: Mathematician turned quant. Founded Renaissance Technologies. The internal Medallion Fund averaged ~40% net annually for decades. Public 13F shows the slower Institutional Equities fund.

description: Reflexivity theorist. Famous for breaking the Bank of England in 1992.
bio: Founder of Soros Fund Management. Built one of the great macro track records — the Quantum Fund. Made $1B+ shorting GBP on Black Wednesday. Now primarily a family office focused on philanthropy.

description: Endowment portfolio managed conservatively for long-horizon philanthropy.
bio: The investment trust funding the Gates Foundation. Long-term, conservative allocation with notable concentration in Berkshire-influenced staples and Microsoft.

description: Contrarian value, famous for beating the S&P 500 for 15 straight years at Legg Mason.
bio: Manages Miller Value Partners. The only manager to beat the S&P 500 fifteen calendar years in a row (1991-2005). Long-time Amazon and bitcoin bull.

description: Quant + options market-maker. Game theory roots. Very large 13F due to market-making positions.
bio: Co-founder of SIG. Trader-driven, options-focused. Big positions in TikTok parent ByteDance pre-disposal. 13F often shows market-maker inventory — read carefully.

description: Multi-strategy giant. Best-performing hedge fund of all time by absolute dollar returns.
bio: Founder of Citadel and Citadel Securities. Multi-strategy hedge fund — equity, fixed income, commodities, credit, quant. Made $16B in 2022 alone, the largest annual fund gain ever.

description: Stock-picker. Multi-manager platform. Owner of the New York Mets.
bio: Founder of Point72 (formerly SAC). Multi-manager equity hedge fund. Famous trading floor instinct. Owns the Mets.

description: Original Tiger fund. Mentored an entire generation of hedge fund founders ('Tiger Cubs').
bio: Founder of Tiger Management. Long-short fundamental investor. Seeded a generation of star managers (Coleman, Mandel, Halvorsen, McGuire) collectively called the Tiger Cubs. Passed in 2022.

description: Tech and internet specialist. Long-term concentrated growth.
bio: Founder of Tiger Global. Tiger Cub. Made his career on early bets on internet leaders globally (JD.com, Facebook, Spotify). Aggressive growth/late-stage venture style.

description: Long-short equity. Quality compounders. Tiger Cub.
bio: Co-founder of Viking Global. Tiger Cub. Long-short equity emphasizing quality compounders, deep fundamental work.

description: Long-short equity. Tiger Cub.
bio: Founder of Maverick Capital. Tiger Cub. Sector-specialist analyst structure; concentrated long-short equity.

description: Retired but iconic. 'Invest in what you know'. 29% annualized over 13 years at Magellan.
bio: Ran Fidelity Magellan 1977-1990, returning ~29% annualized. Author of One Up On Wall Street. Retired from active management, advises Fidelity. No 13F — included as reference profile only.

description: Former House Speaker. Highly-followed STOCK Act disclosures, often big-tech and options.
bio: U.S. Representative (CA-11), former Speaker. Trades are disclosed via STOCK Act PTRs within 30-45 days. Her husband Paul (a venture investor) makes most filings; the pair are notorious for well-timed mega-cap and options bets.

description: Senator with notable agricultural and futures trades surfaced by STOCK Act tracking.
bio: U.S. Senator (R-AL). His PTRs have flagged frequent futures and individual-stock activity, including positions in companies with relevant policy exposure.

description: House Rep with frequent STOCK Act disclosures.
bio: U.S. Representative (TX-02). Periodic active trader; PTRs aggregated by Capitol Trades / Quiver.

description: Silicon Valley district. Disclosures show concentrated tech holdings.
bio: U.S. Representative (CA-17 — Silicon Valley). PTRs are disclosed via the House clerk; book skews to large-cap tech given district focus.

description: House Rep whose trade timing around policy events has drawn scrutiny.
bio: U.S. Representative (GA-14). PTRs publicly disclosed; multiple instances of trade timing around legislative votes have surfaced in press coverage.
